meaning: n.塞莉斯泰因(又译塞莉斯廷,Celeste的昵称)(f.)
examples: This paper shows the test results of certain celestine ore.
Celestine is a young mouse who lives in the underground world of rodents.
Therefore, the author considers the deposit to be a sedimentary-transform type of celestine.
Celestine V, a hermit who founded the religious order that bears his name, was later canonized.
It seems, like Celestine V and maybe even Paul VI, that Benedict was never quite comfortable as pope.
